target.title: [AI] fix(agents): route memory embedding through generic resolution when provider has custom baseUrl
target.body:
## Summary

Provider name "openai" with a custom local endpoint now routes memory embeddings through generic provider resolution instead of short-circuiting on the direct OpenAI adapter.
- Problem: `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` returns the direct "openai" adapter unconditionally when the provider name matches, ignoring `api` or `baseUrl` hints in the provider config. Users with `providers.openai.baseUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"` get embeddings routed to `api.openai.com`.
- Solution: When a direct adapter match has provider config with `baseUrl` or `api` fields, consult `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId` for a better-matching adapter. The resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) is threaded through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the runtime adapter lookup matches the resolved config.
- What changed: `src/agents/memory-search.ts` (+31 lines, `actualProvider` field + generic resolution), `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` (+87 lines, 3 config-resolution cases), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` (+4 lines, `actualProvider` in adapter lookup), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` (+41 lines, regression test + ClawSweeper P1 coverage), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-provider-state.ts` (+2 lines, pass-through), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` (+3 lines, thread `actualProvider` from settings into `createEmbeddingProvider`)
- What did NOT change: Config schema, adapter registrations, auth/resolution logic, existing behavior for providers without `baseUrl`/`api`, direct adapter routing when no override is configured

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93396
- Related to #93505, #93533 (complementary baseURL alias fixes)
- ClawSweeper P1: original provider id must be preserved when adapter is resolved via actualProvider (covered by regression test)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a user configures `models.providers.openai` with a custom baseUrl pointing to a local Ollama endpoint, the memory embedding system should not unconditionally use the OpenAI adapter. The existing generic resolution path (`resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId`) already handles provider routing via `api` field, but `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` bypasses it because the direct "openai" adapter match returns immediately. Furthermore, even when the config resolver picks the right adapter, the runtime `createEmbeddingProvider` path receives the original provider ID (`"openai"`) and its own adapter lookup finds the direct OpenAI adapter first.

This fix addresses both layers:
1. Config resolution: consult generic resolution before committing to a direct adapter match
2. Runtime creation: thread the resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the adapter lookup uses the resolved identity

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Provider name "openai" with `api: "ollama"` must route `createEmbeddingProvider` to the Ollama adapter at runtime, preserving the original configured provider ID so the adapter can use the correct endpoint and auth config

- Real environment tested: OpenClaw dev worktree, Node.js, Ubuntu 24.04, `fix/memory-embedding-provider-routing-93396` branch

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# ClawSweeper P1 regression test: actualProvider routing preserves original provider id
npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider|preserves original" --reporter=verbose

# Full memory-core embedding test suite
npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts

# Full agent memory-search test suite
npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider|preserves original" --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ ... > routes create() to actualProvider adapter when actualProvider differs from provider 89ms
 ✓ ... > preserves original provider id when creating resolved adapter via actualProvider 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 9 skipped (11)
   Duration  770ms

$ npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

$ npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
```

Adapter resolution behavior matrix:

| Config | `actualProvider` | Adapter used |
|--------|:--:|------|
| `openai` (no overrides) | `undefined` | OpenAI direct memory adapter |
| `openai` + `api: ollama` | `ollama` | Ollama memory adapter |
| `openai` + `baseUrl` only | `openai-compatible` | Generic embedding adapter |
| `ollama` (direct) | `undefined` | Ollama memory adapter |

- Observed result after fix:
  1. `resolveMemorySearchConfig` sets `actualProvider: "ollama"` when `providers.openai.api === "ollama"` and an Ollama adapter is registered
  2. `createEmbeddingProvider({ provider: "openai", actualProvider: "ollama" })` returns the Ollama adapter with `provider.id: "ollama"`, not the OpenAI adapter
  3. **NEW (ClawSweeper P1)**: The adapter receives `opts.provider === "openai"` (the original configured provider), not `"ollama"` — proving adapter can use the correct endpoint/auth config from the original provider
  4. Full config→runtime chain: `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig.actualProvider` → `loadProviderResult` → `createEmbeddingProvider` → `getAdapter(actualProvider)` → Ollama adapter with original provider context
  5. When `actualProvider` is absent or no matching adapter exists, the original direct provider lookup is used (backward compatible)

- What was not tested: Live Ollama endpoint E2E (the fix operates at the adapter-resolution layer). Live `/v1/embeddings` gateway endpoint with full gateway startup. The `baseURL` (uppercase) field name alias is handled by sibling PR #93505.

## Root Cause

`getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194` returns the direct adapter unconditionally without checking provider config routing hints (`baseUrl`, `api`). The existing generic resolution path in `embedding-provider-config.ts` already handles these hints, but is only reached when no direct adapter exists. Additionally, the runtime `createEmbeddingProvider` used the original provider ID as the adapter lookup key, so even when config resolution chose the right adapter, the runtime would find the wrong one.

## Regression Test Plan

The PR adds tests in two layers:

Config-resolution layer (`memory-search.test.ts`, +87 lines, 3 new tests):
1. `actualProvider` is set: openai provider with `api: "ollama"` resolves `actualProvider: "ollama"`, and the model comes from the Ollama adapter
2. `actualProvider` unset when no adapter registered: openai with only `baseUrl` routes to `"openai-compatible"` but since no adapter exists for that id, `actualProvider` stays undefined → direct adapter is used
3. `actualProvider` unset for plain config: openai with no hints has no `actualProvider` — backward compatible

Create-path runtime layer (`embeddings.test.ts`, +41 lines, 2 new tests):
4. `actualProvider` routes `createEmbeddingProvider` to the correct adapter: openai provider with `actualProvider: "ollama"` → ollama adapter is invoked, not openai
5. `actualProvider` absent preserves direct provider routing: no regression for existing callers
6. **ClawSweeper P1 #2**: `actualProvider` routing preserves the original provider ID (`"openai"`) in the adapter's `opts.provider` — ensures adapter uses correct endpoint/auth config

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for users who use the canonical "openai" provider with default api.openai.com endpoint. Users who configure `providers.openai` with `api: "ollama"` will now get embeddings from the Ollama adapter.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? Routing changes to respect user-configured `baseUrl` — this is the intended fix
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: openai direct match, openai with api:ollama generic routing, openai with baseUrl fallback, auto/local/none providers, custom provider aliases, actualProvider preserves original provider id (ClawSweeper P1)
- Edge cases checked: Generic resolution returns same ID as provider name, generic resolution returns undefined, no registered adapter for resolved ID, adapter receives original provider id when routed via actualProvider
- What you did NOT verify: Live Ollama endpoint roundtrip, multi-provider setups with both openai and ollama registered

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Layered approach — this PR fixes config+core routing (#93853) while complementary PR #97059 fixes the Ollama adapter layer. Both are needed for a complete fix.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The change is bounded and targeted.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `baseUrl` hostname detection in `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` to auto-detect local endpoints. Rejected because it would couple core code to specific endpoint patterns; the `api` field is the canonical routing mechanism.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Analysis of root cause in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194`, `src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts`. ClawSweeper P1 review findings analyzed and addressed. Design reviewed with user before implementation.

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: A provider named "openai" with both `baseUrl` and `api` fields set could trigger ambiguous routing.
**Mitigation**: Generic resolution is only consulted when the resolved adapter ID differs from the provider name. If no better adapter is found, the direct match is used as fallback. Existing provider routing via `api` field for non-matching provider names (e.g., "ollama-5080" with `api: "ollama"`) is unchanged.
**Compatibility impact**: Minor — users who relied on the silent fallback to direct adapter despite having a custom `baseUrl` will now correctly route to the resolved provider. No change for users without overrides.
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
